speaker: MARSHAL
text: My Lord Aumerle, is Harry Hereford armed?

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Yea, at all points, and longs to enter in.

speaker: MARSHAL
text: The Duke of Norfolk, sprightfully and bold,

speaker: MARSHAL
text: Stays but the summons of the appellant’s trumpet.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Why then, the champions are prepared and stay

speaker: AUMERLE
text: For nothing but his Majesty’s approach.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Marshal, demand of yonder champion

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The cause of his arrival here in arms,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Ask him his name, and orderly proceed

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To swear him in the justice of his cause.

speaker: MARSHAL
text: In God’s name and the King’s, say who thou art

speaker: MARSHAL
text: And why thou comest thus knightly clad in arms,

speaker: MARSHAL
text: Against what man thou com’st, and what thy quarrel.

speaker: MARSHAL
text: Speak truly on thy knighthood and thy oath,

speaker: MARSHAL
text: As so defend thee heaven and thy valor.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: My name is Thomas Mowbray, Duke of Norfolk,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Who hither come engagèd by my oath—

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Which God defend a knight should violate!—

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Both to defend my loyalty and truth

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: To God, my king, and my succeeding issue,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Against the Duke of Hereford that appeals me,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And by the grace of God and this mine arm

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: To prove him, in defending of myself,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: A traitor to my God, my king, and me;

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And as I truly fight, defend me heaven.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Marshal, ask yonder knight in arms

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Both who he is and why he cometh hither

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Thus plated in habiliments of war,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And formally, according to our law,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Depose him in the justice of his cause.

speaker: MARSHAL
text: What is thy name? And wherefore com’st thou hither,

speaker: MARSHAL
text: Before King Richard in his royal lists?

speaker: MARSHAL
text: Against whom comest thou? And what’s thy quarrel?

speaker: MARSHAL
text: Speak like a true knight, so defend thee heaven.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Harry of Hereford, Lancaster, and Derby

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Am I, who ready here do stand in arms

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: To prove, by God’s grace and my body’s valor,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: In lists, on Thomas Mowbray, Duke of Norfolk,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: That he is a traitor foul and dangerous

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: To God of heaven, King Richard, and to me.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And as I truly fight, defend me heaven.

speaker: MARSHAL
text: On pain of death, no person be so bold

speaker: MARSHAL
text: Or daring-hardy as to touch the lists,

speaker: MARSHAL
text: Except the Marshal and such officers

speaker: MARSHAL
text: Appointed to direct these fair designs.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Lord Marshal, let me kiss my sovereign’s hand

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And bow my knee before his Majesty;

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: For Mowbray and myself are like two men

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: That vow a long and weary pilgrimage.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Then let us take a ceremonious leave

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And loving farewell of our several friends.

speaker: MARSHAL
text: The appellant in all duty greets your Highness

speaker: MARSHAL
text: And craves to kiss your hand and take his leave.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: We will descend and fold him in our arms.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Cousin of Hereford, as thy cause is right,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: So be thy fortune in this royal fight.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Farewell, my blood—which, if today thou shed,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Lament we may but not revenge thee dead.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: O, let no noble eye profane a tear

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: For me if I be gored with Mowbray’s spear.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: As confident as is the falcon’s flight

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Against a bird do I with Mowbray fight.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: My loving lord, I take my leave of you.—

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Of you, my noble cousin, Lord Aumerle;

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Not sick, although I have to do with death,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: But lusty, young, and cheerly drawing breath.—

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Lo, as at English feasts, so I regreet

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: The daintiest last, to make the end most sweet.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: O, thou the earthly author of my blood,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Whose youthful spirit in me regenerate

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Doth with a twofold vigor lift me up

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: To reach at victory above my head,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Add proof unto mine armor with thy prayers,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And with thy blessings steel my lance’s point

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: That it may enter Mowbray’s waxen coat

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And furbish new the name of John o’ Gaunt,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Even in the lusty havior of his son.

speaker: GAUNT
text: God in thy good cause make thee prosperous.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Be swift like lightning in the execution,

speaker: GAUNT
text: And let thy blows, doubly redoubled,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Fall like amazing thunder on the casque

speaker: GAUNT
text: Of thy adverse pernicious enemy.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Rouse up thy youthful blood, be valiant, and live.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Mine innocence and Saint George to thrive!

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: However God or fortune cast my lot,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: There lives or dies, true to King Richard’s throne,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: A loyal, just, and upright gentleman.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Never did captive with a freer heart

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Cast off his chains of bondage and embrace

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: His golden uncontrolled enfranchisement

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: More than my dancing soul doth celebrate

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: This feast of battle with mine adversary.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Most mighty liege and my companion peers,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Take from my mouth the wish of happy years.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: As gentle and as jocund as to jest

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Go I to fight. Truth hath a quiet breast.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Farewell, my lord. Securely I espy

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Virtue with valor couchèd in thine eye.—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Order the trial, marshal, and begin.

speaker: MARSHAL
text: Harry of Hereford, Lancaster, and Derby,

speaker: MARSHAL
text: Receive thy lance; and God defend the right.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Strong as a tower in hope, I cry “Amen!”

speaker: MARSHAL
text: Go bear this lance to Thomas, Duke of Norfolk.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              HERALD
text: Harry of Hereford, Lancaster, and Derby

speaker:
FIRST
               
              HERALD
text: Stands here for God, his sovereign, and himself,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              HERALD
text: On pain to be found false and recreant,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              HERALD
text: To prove the Duke of Norfolk, Thomas Mowbray,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              HERALD
text: A traitor to his God, his king, and him,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              HERALD
text: And dares him to set forward to the fight.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              HERALD
text: Here standeth Thomas Mowbray, Duke of Norfolk,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              HERALD
text: On pain to be found false and recreant,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              HERALD
text: Both to defend himself and to approve

speaker:
SECOND
               
              HERALD
text: Henry of Hereford, Lancaster, and Derby

speaker:
SECOND
               
              HERALD
text: To God, his sovereign, and to him disloyal,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              HERALD
text: Courageously and with a free desire

speaker:
SECOND
               
              HERALD
text: Attending but the signal to begin.

speaker: MARSHAL
text: Sound, trumpets, and set forward, combatants.

speaker: MARSHAL
text: Stay! The King hath thrown his warder down.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Let them lay by their helmets and their spears,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And both return back to their chairs again.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text:
To
                 
                his
                 
                council
                .
                 
              Withdraw with us, and let the trumpets sound

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: While we return these dukes what we decree.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text:
To
                 
                Bolingbroke
                 
                and
                 
                Mowbray
                .
                 
              Draw near,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And list what with our council we have done.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: For that our kingdom’s earth should not be soiled

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: With that dear blood which it hath fosterèd;

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And for our eyes do hate the dire aspect

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Of civil wounds plowed up with neighbor’s sword;

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And for we think the eagle-wingèd pride

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Of sky-aspiring and ambitious thoughts,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: With rival-hating envy, set on you

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To wake our peace, which in our country’s cradle

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Draws the sweet infant breath of gentle sleep,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Which, so roused up with boist’rous untuned drums,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: With harsh resounding trumpets’ dreadful bray,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And grating shock of wrathful iron arms,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Might from our quiet confines fright fair peace

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And make us wade even in our kindred’s blood:

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Therefore we banish you our territories.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: You, cousin Hereford, upon pain of life,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Till twice five summers have enriched our fields

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Shall not regreet our fair dominions,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: But tread the stranger paths of banishment.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Your will be done. This must my comfort be:

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: That sun that warms you here shall shine on me,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And those his golden beams to you here lent

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Shall point on me and gild my banishment.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Norfolk, for thee remains a heavier doom,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Which I with some unwillingness pronounce:

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The sly, slow hours shall not determinate

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The dateless limit of thy dear exile.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The hopeless word of “never to return”

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Breathe I against thee, upon pain of life.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: A heavy sentence, my most sovereign liege,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And all unlooked-for from your Highness’ mouth.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: A dearer merit, not so deep a maim

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: As to be cast forth in the common air,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Have I deservèd at your Highness’ hands.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: The language I have learnt these forty years,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: My native English, now I must forgo;

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And now my tongue’s use is to me no more

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Than an unstringèd viol or a harp,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Or like a cunning instrument cased up,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Or, being open, put into his hands

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: That knows no touch to tune the harmony.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Within my mouth you have enjailed my tongue,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Doubly portcullised with my teeth and lips,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And dull unfeeling barren ignorance

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Is made my jailor to attend on me.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: I am too old to fawn upon a nurse,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Too far in years to be a pupil now.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: What is thy sentence then but speechless death,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Which robs my tongue from breathing native breath?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: It boots thee not to be compassionate.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: After our sentence plaining comes too late.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Then thus I turn me from my country’s light,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: To dwell in solemn shades of endless night.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Return again, and take an oath with thee.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text:
To
                 
                Mowbray
                 
                and
                 
                Bolingbroke
                .
                 
              Lay on our royal sword your banished hands.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Swear by the duty that you owe to God—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Our part therein we banish with yourselves—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To keep the oath that we administer:

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: You never shall, so help you truth and God,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Embrace each other’s love in banishment,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Nor never look upon each other’s face,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Nor never write, regreet, nor reconcile

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: This louring tempest of your homebred hate,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Nor never by advisèd purpose meet

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To plot, contrive, or complot any ill

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: ’Gainst us, our state, our subjects, or our land.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: I swear.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And I, to keep all this.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Norfolk, so far as to mine enemy:

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: By this time, had the King permitted us,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: One of our souls had wandered in the air,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Banished this frail sepulcher of our flesh,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: As now our flesh is banished from this land.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Confess thy treasons ere thou fly the realm.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Since thou hast far to go, bear not along

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: The clogging burden of a guilty soul.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: No, Bolingbroke; if ever I were traitor,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: My name be blotted from the book of life,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And I from heaven banished as from hence.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: But what thou art, God, thou, and I do know,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And all too soon, I fear, the King shall rue.—

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Farewell, my liege. Now no way can I stray;

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Save back to England, all the world’s my way.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Uncle, even in the glasses of thine eyes

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: I see thy grievèd heart. Thy sad aspect

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Hath from the number of his banished years

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text:
Plucked four away.
                 
                To
                 
                Bolingbroke
                .
               Six frozen winters spent,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Return with welcome home from banishment.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: How long a time lies in one little word!

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Four lagging winters and four wanton springs

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: End in a word; such is the breath of kings.

speaker: GAUNT
text: I thank my liege that in regard of me

speaker: GAUNT
text: He shortens four years of my son’s exile.

speaker: GAUNT
text: But little vantage shall I reap thereby;

speaker: GAUNT
text: For, ere the six years that he hath to spend

speaker: GAUNT
text: Can change their moons and bring their times about,

speaker: GAUNT
text: My oil-dried lamp and time-bewasted light

speaker: GAUNT
text: Shall be extinct with age and endless night;

speaker: GAUNT
text: My inch of taper will be burnt and done,

speaker: GAUNT
text: And blindfold death not let me see my son.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Why, uncle, thou hast many years to live.

speaker: GAUNT
text: But not a minute, king, that thou canst give.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Shorten my days thou canst with sullen sorrow,

speaker: GAUNT
text: And pluck nights from me, but not lend a morrow.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Thou canst help time to furrow me with age,

speaker: GAUNT
text: But stop no wrinkle in his pilgrimage.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Thy word is current with him for my death,

speaker: GAUNT
text: But dead, thy kingdom cannot buy my breath.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Thy son is banished upon good advice,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Whereto thy tongue a party verdict gave.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Why at our justice seem’st thou then to lour?

speaker: GAUNT
text: Things sweet to taste prove in digestion sour.

speaker: GAUNT
text: You urged me as a judge, but I had rather

speaker: GAUNT
text: You would have bid me argue like a father.

speaker: GAUNT
text: O, had it been a stranger, not my child,

speaker: GAUNT
text: To smooth his fault I should have been more mild.

speaker: GAUNT
text: A partial slander sought I to avoid,

speaker: GAUNT
text: And in the sentence my own life destroyed.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Alas, I looked when some of you should say

speaker: GAUNT
text: I was too strict, to make mine own away.

speaker: GAUNT
text: But you gave leave to my unwilling tongue

speaker: GAUNT
text: Against my will to do myself this wrong.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Cousin, farewell.—And, uncle, bid him so.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Six years we banish him, and he shall go.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Cousin, farewell. What presence must not know,

speaker: AUMERLE
text: From where you do remain let paper show.

speaker: MARSHAL
text: My lord, no leave take I, for I will ride,

speaker: MARSHAL
text: As far as land will let me, by your side.

speaker: GAUNT
text: O, to what purpose dost thou hoard thy words,

speaker: GAUNT
text: That thou returnest no greeting to thy friends?

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: I have too few to take my leave of you,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: When the tongue’s office should be prodigal

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: To breathe the abundant dolor of the heart.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Thy grief is but thy absence for a time.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Joy absent, grief is present for that time.

speaker: GAUNT
text: What is six winters? They are quickly gone.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: To men in joy; but grief makes one hour ten.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Call it a travel that thou tak’st for pleasure.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: My heart will sigh when I miscall it so,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Which finds it an enforcèd pilgrimage.

speaker: GAUNT
text: The sullen passage of thy weary steps

speaker: GAUNT
text: Esteem as foil wherein thou art to set

speaker: GAUNT
text: The precious jewel of thy home return.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Nay, rather every tedious stride I make

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Will but remember me what a deal of world

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: I wander from the jewels that I love.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Must I not serve a long apprenticehood

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: To foreign passages, and in the end,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Having my freedom, boast of nothing else

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: But that I was a journeyman to grief?

speaker: GAUNT
text: All places that the eye of heaven visits

speaker: GAUNT
text: Are to a wise man ports and happy havens.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Teach thy necessity to reason thus:

speaker: GAUNT
text: There is no virtue like necessity.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Think not the King did banish thee,

speaker: GAUNT
text: But thou the King. Woe doth the heavier sit

speaker: GAUNT
text: Where it perceives it is but faintly borne.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Go, say I sent thee forth to purchase honor,

speaker: GAUNT
text: And not the King exiled thee; or suppose

speaker: GAUNT
text: Devouring pestilence hangs in our air

speaker: GAUNT
text: And thou art flying to a fresher clime.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Look what thy soul holds dear, imagine it

speaker: GAUNT
text: To lie that way thou goest, not whence thou com’st.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Suppose the singing birds musicians,

speaker: GAUNT
text: The grass whereon thou tread’st the presence strewed,

speaker: GAUNT
text: The flowers fair ladies, and thy steps no more

speaker: GAUNT
text: Than a delightful measure or a dance;

speaker: GAUNT
text: For gnarling sorrow hath less power to bite

speaker: GAUNT
text: The man that mocks at it and sets it light.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: O, who can hold a fire in his hand

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: By thinking on the frosty Caucasus?

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Or cloy the hungry edge of appetite

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: By bare imagination of a feast?

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Or wallow naked in December snow

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: By thinking on fantastic summer’s heat?

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: O no, the apprehension of the good

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Gives but the greater feeling to the worse.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Fell sorrow’s tooth doth never rankle more

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Than when he bites but lanceth not the sore.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Come, come, my son, I’ll bring thee on thy way.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Had I thy youth and cause, I would not stay.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Then, England’s ground, farewell; sweet soil, adieu,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: My mother and my nurse that bears me yet.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Where’er I wander, boast of this I can,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Though banished, yet a trueborn Englishman.
